content.full_text:
•

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 8, 2004

Thomas W. Hiltachk
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-204

Dear Mr. Hiltachk:

This letter is to notify you that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for advice on behalf of Oakland Harbor Partners, LLC and Signature Properties regarding section 84308 of the Political Reform Act.¹ This is your second request for advice on this topic. The first question, which was almost identical to this inquiry, was withdrawn on September 15, 2004, because it was determined to be a request for third party advice and included facts relating to past conduct of the party. We still cannot provide you with advice as this request also involves the duties of third parties.

The duties of a "participant" under section 84308 concern the prohibition on the making of contributions either during or for the three months following a final decision. You have stated that the reason for your inquiry is for a determination of whether a contribution under section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers, specifically Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly, and whether such a contribution should be made by a "participant" you represent. Although we understand that this knowledge would assist your client in deciding whether or not to make a contribution, the advice would be a determination of the rights and responsibilities of Lt. Governor Bustamante or Controller Westly under the Act, and not the "participant" you represent. Should either Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante or Controller Steve Westly authorize you to request advice on his behalf, then we would be happy to provide you with the advice you seek. Pursuant to regulation 18329(b)(3)(B)(copy enclosed), written advice or assistance is not provided in response to unauthorized third party requests.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-04-204
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [Galena West signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

GW:jg

Enclosure

I:\AdviceLtrs\W-04-204

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a contribution under Political Reform Act section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers (Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly), and whether such a contribution should be made by a represented participant.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because it constitutes an unauthorized third-party request seeking to determine the rights and responsibilities of the state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant, and such advice cannot be provided without authorization from the affected officers themselves.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not a standard advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist. The document explains the reason for withdrawal and references a prior identical withdrawn request from September 15, 2004. Legal question and conclusion are synthesized from the body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a contribution under Political Reform Act section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers (Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly), and whether such a contribution should be made by a represented participant.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because it constitutes an unauthorized third-party request seeking to determine the rights and responsibilities of the state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant, and such advice cannot be provided without authorization from the affected officers themselves.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request from an attorney regarding Political Reform Act section 84308 contribution restrictions, determining it was an impermissible third-party request that would require determining the duties of state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant.
